project.name: Mini-IPS
project.purpose: libpcap-based inline-capable mini IPS that captures TCP packets, reassembles HTTP streams, evaluates request/response content against CRS-derived regex signatures, logs detections, and injects TCP RST packets to disrupt offending flows.

high_level_flow: main() parses CLI flags for interface, BPF, mode, and regex backend.
driver_init() creates one capture queue per worker and one worker thread per CPU core, capped at 64.
capture_thread_func() polls libpcap and hashes each IPv4 TCP/UDP packet to a worker queue using a symmetric 5-tuple hash.
worker_thread_func() dequeues packets and calls on_packet() with the worker-specific app_ctx_t.
on_packet() optionally snapshots TCP session state for RST/debug logging, then feeds packets to httgw_ingest_packet() in sniffing mode.
httgw_ingest_packet() parses Ethernet/VLAN/IPv4/TCP, normalizes the flow key, updates per-flow TCP state, and passes payload bytes into TCP reassembly.
reasm_ingest() buffers out-of-order segments per direction, flushes contiguous bytes, and invokes on_stream_data() when reassembled application data becomes available.
on_stream_data() appends bytes into the directional HTTP parser. Completed HTTP messages are drained and dispatched to on_request() or on_response().
run_detect() evaluates URI, query args, arg names, headers, and body against loaded signatures. It accumulates score from is_high_priority matches and treats score >= 5 as a blocking detection.
on_request()/on_response() write structured logs and call request_rst_both() on detection.
request_rst_both() computes current sequence/ack values from httgw session snapshot and emits five TCP RST+ACK packets in each direction via raw L3 send.

threading_model.worker_thread_count: equal to online CPU count, bounded to [1, 64]

queueing.full_queue_behavior: blocking by default in runtime driver_init(); packet_ring_enq() waits when queue is full

threading_model.worker_isolation: Each worker owns its own detect engine, httgw instance, and raw RST tx context. Shared logging and counters live in app_shared_t.
threading_model.ordering_guarantee: Packets belonging to the same symmetric 5-tuple are routed to the same worker, preserving per-flow ordering relative to capture order.

major_modules.name: main application
responsibilities: Parse CLI options and environment flags
Initialize capture, workers, logging, detection engines, and HTTP gateways
Bridge packet driver callbacks to HTTP parsing and detection callbacks
Emit structured logs
Control graceful shutdown on SIGINT

important_functions.app_log_open: Opens LOG_FILE or logs/ips.log and initializes log mutex.
important_functions.app_log_write: Writes structured key=value log lines with timestamp and level.
important_functions.app_log_attack: Writes WARN detect log including attack name, score, matched_rules, matched_texts, and detection latency.
important_functions.run_detect: Core application-level detection pipeline over parsed HTTP message fields.
important_functions.on_request: Handles parsed HTTP requests. Logs detect/pass/error outcomes and requests RST on detection.
important_functions.on_response: Same as on_request but for response messages; intentionally skipped when mode is sniffing.
important_functions.on_packet: Per-worker packet callback. Saves pre-RST session snapshots for later logging and feeds packet into httgw.
important_functions.request_rst_both: Fetches session snapshot, verifies bidirectional visibility, logs rst_skip or rst_request, and injects RST in both directions.
important_functions.main: Bootstraps the full runtime, starts driver threads, and idles until SIGINT.

major_modules.name: packet capture driver
responsibilities: Wrap libpcap setup and activation
Implement per-worker packet rings
Spawn capture and worker threads
Distribute packets by normalized flow hash

data_structures.packet_slot_t: Fixed-size packet buffer with capture timestamp.
data_structures.packet_queue_set_t: Array of packet rings, one per worker.
data_structures.driver_runtime_t: Runtime state for capture thread, worker threads, queues, and callbacks.

behavior_notes: Packet distribution falls back to round-robin when packet is not IPv4 TCP/UDP or cannot be parsed.
capture_poll_once() copies packet bytes out of libpcap-owned memory before enqueueing.
driver_set_packet_handler_multi() assigns per-worker user pointers, which main uses for per-worker app_ctx_t.

major_modules.name: HTTP gateway and TCP reassembly
responsibilities: Parse Ethernet/VLAN/IPv4/TCP headers
Normalize bidirectional flow keys
Track per-flow TCP sequence/window state for later RST injection
Reassemble TCP payload streams in both directions
Own per-direction HTTP stream parsers
Emit parsed HTTP messages to callbacks
Construct and send raw TCP RST packets

internal_layers.details: httgw_session holds flow key, two http_stream_t handles, base/last/next seq numbers, last ACKs, window scale, FIN flags, and RST flags. sess_get_or_create_internal() lazily allocates sessions.
reasm_ctx maintains a separate hash table keyed by normalized flow. Each direction tracks next_seq, pending segment linked list, segment count, byte budget, and FIN/RST markers.
Contiguous bytes flushed from reassembly are passed to on_stream_data(), which feeds the proper directional http_stream_t and drains fully parsed messages.

sequence_tracking_rules: First packet seen in a direction establishes base_seq_ab/base_seq_ba.
next_seq_ab/next_seq_ba are derived by tcp_next_seq(seq, payload_len, flags), so SYN and FIN consume sequence space.
last_ack and advertised window are only updated when ACK is not stale relative to prior state.
Window scale is only learned from SYN packets with TCP option kind 3.
reassembly_rules: REASM_MODE_LATE_START allows midstream starts if payload resembles HTTP request/response start-line.
REASM_MODE_STRICT_SYN requires observing SYN before accepting stream data.
Segments are stored in a sorted linked list, overlap is trimmed, duplicates are dropped, and contiguous adjacent segments are merged.
Resource limits: 4096 sessions, 1024 segments per direction, 12 MiB queued bytes per direction, 60 second idle timeout.
On reassembly insert failure, the session is removed rather than kept in a potentially inconsistent state.

behavior: RST packets are sent as raw IPv4 TCP packets without Ethernet header.
Code sends a burst of 5 identical RST+ACK packets per direction.
Sequence/ack values come from the current or cached session snapshot.
main.c refuses to send until both directions have been seen, logging rst_skip otherwise.

major_modules.name: HTTP stream parser
responsibilities: Maintain directional byte buffer
Parse HTTP start line, headers, and body
Support Content-Length and chunked transfer encoding
Queue parsed http_message_t objects for later polling
parser_behavior: Messages are parsed only after CRLFCRLF terminates headers.
Requests and responses share one parser; parse_start_line() distinguishes by leading HTTP/.
Headers are stored raw excluding the start line, enabling later header scans without reparsing the full buffer.
Chunked bodies are dechunked into a contiguous body buffer.
Malformed messages return EPROTO and cause the caller to reset the stream state.
Default max buffer and body sizes are both 12 MiB.
known_limits: No persistent response body streaming without explicit Content-Length or chunked encoding.

major_modules.name: Detection facade
responsibilities: Load signatures on first use
Select either all signatures or a single policy subset
Own engine_runtime_t and expose simplified match APIs
Collect matched rules with elapsed time accounting
behavior_notes: detect_engine_create("ALL", DETECT_JIT_AUTO) is what main uses for every worker.
Context validation is strict: only predefined IPS_CTX_* values are accepted.
last_err is stored per detect_engine instance.

major_modules.name: Regex engine backend
responsibilities: Compile signatures using PCRE or Hyperscan
Evaluate payloads and collect first/all matches
Expose backend name and JIT status

PCRE: Compiles only rules with op == IPS_OP_RX.
Supports JIT auto/on/off. DETECT_JIT_ON hard-fails if JIT is unavailable.
Collects actual matched substring offsets from pcre_exec().
Hyperscan: Builds one hs_database_t per IPS context.
Uses hs_compile_multi() over only rx operators matching that context.
Does not recover actual matched substring text; collect path appends empty matched_text.
Single hs_scratch is stored in runtime and reused for scans.

profiling_hooks: IPS_PROFILE_REGEX enables stderr profiling for slow regex executions.
IPS_PROFILE_THRESHOLD_US sets the minimum elapsed microseconds before logging.

major_modules.name: Signature loader
responsibilities: Load JSONL CRS-derived signatures into global arrays
Parse per-line JSON fields without an external JSON library
Map policy, context, and operator strings to enums
major_modules.important_caveat: The engine currently executes only op == rx patterns. Many parsed CRS operators are loaded into memory but not semantically enforced.

major_modules.name: DB ingest and viewer
responsibilities: Convert structured log lines into SQLite rows
Continuously tail the IPS log file and insert new events
Serve a tiny HTML/JSON viewer over the SQLite database
pipeline: parse_kv_line() tokenizes shell-like key=value lines with shlex.split().
should_store() keeps detect, rst_request, stream_error, detect_error, and all ERROR-level lines.
init_db() creates ips_events and lazily adds newer columns for compatibility.
follow_ingest.py can tail from file end and prints only rst_request ingest notices.
web.py provides / for HTML and /api/events for JSON.

implemented_contexts.source: query string names and form-urlencoded body names
query string values and form-urlencoded body values

detection_pipeline.response_handling: on_response() calls run_detect(), but run_detect() is request-oriented and does not evaluate IPS_CTX_RESPONSE_BODY. Response detections therefore only occur if response data happens to match request-scoped contexts or if code is later extended.
decoding_behavior: detect_match_decoded() performs a first pass on raw bytes.
If payload contains % or +, it URL-decodes into a temporary buffer and runs detection again on the decoded form.
Decoded matches are appended to the same detect_match_list_t.

scoring.source: sum of rule->is_high_priority for each newly appended match

app_shared_t.ownership: single shared instance across all workers

logging_contract.format: single-line key=value records with quoted string values where needed
notes: DB ingest retains rst_request and detection-related events, not all pass traffic by default.
debug TCP packet lines are written to stderr, not to the structured log file.

supported_modes.details: Packets are parsed, detected, logged, and can trigger active RST injection.
CLI accepts the mode, but on_packet() contains only TODO comments.
CLI accepts the mode, but on_packet() contains only TODO comments. on_response() also returns early unless mode is non-sniff.

covered_areas: HTTP parser Content-Length, chunked transfer, reset-after-error
TCP/HTTP ingest with duplicate segment handling
RST packet construction and sequence/ack logic
Regex compatibility tooling and RID-focused benchmarking
gaps: No test demonstrating end-to-end main() startup with libpcap capture threads.
No test covering Hyperscan collect semantics versus PCRE collect semantics.
No test exercising response-body detection path, which is currently not wired in run_detect().
No test for DB ingest/web scripts.

important_gaps_and_risks.detail: regex_load_signatures() parses many CRS operators, but engine_runtime only executes rx rules. pm, contains, beginsWith, detectSQLi, and others are not enforced.
run_detect() does not inspect IPS_CTX_RESPONSE_BODY. Response-side scanning is therefore incomplete despite response callbacks and IPS context definitions.
tp/syn and reverse modes are placeholders and should not be treated as production-ready.
Only IPv4 TCP is parsed for HTTP inspection. UDP is only used for queue hashing fallback in driver.c.
driver packet slots cap copied packets at 2048 bytes. Oversized captured packets fail enqueue with EMSGSIZE.
httgw maintains both a TCP state session table and a separate reassembly session table keyed by the same normalized flow. They must stay logically aligned.

summary_for_future_agents: The production path is: libpcap capture -> symmetric flow hashing -> per-worker packet queue -> httgw TCP state update + reassembly -> directional HTTP parser -> run_detect over URI/args/headers/body -> structured log -> optional TCP RST burst -> optional SQLite ingest and web viewing. The most important implementation caveats are that only sniff mode is real, only rx operators are enforced, and response-body detection is not fully wired.
